subject: Sociology
title: Functions of Deviance
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Functions of Deviance.

hook.text: Crime is terrible, right? Durkheim said wait — a society without crime would actually be unhealthy.

buildup.text: Durkheim argued that deviance is functional. It clarifies moral boundaries by showing where the line is.

discovery.text: Punishing deviants unites the community. Shared outrage strengthens collective conscience and solidarity.

twist.text: Deviance can also drive social change. Today's criminal act may become tomorrow's civil right.

climax.text: A society with zero deviance would be so rigid that growth and adaptation become impossible.

quiz.question: Why did Durkheim say deviance is functional?
options: It clarifies moral boundaries and promotes solidarity
